purpose: Generalization test #2 on a 'richer' topic chosen by the user. Candy's rankable axes sit in genuinely different categories (retail sales, corporate revenue, trade/export, per-capita consumption, production scale, history, seasonal), while the answer set (famous brands/candies) stays listable. Rankings only — no 'who invented X' recall. Every fact web-verified (per the silver call-out). NOT filed to the library.
category_spread: retail sales x4 (cap), corporate revenue, export/trade, consumption x2, production x2, history, seasonal — no super-category over a third

candidates.q: Which candy brand sells more in the United States than any other?
candidates.note: $3.17B / 8.2% share in 2024 — defeats the Hershey/Snickers/M&M's reflex. Finalists Reese's, Snickers, M&M's, Hershey, KitKat.

candidates.q: Which chocolate bar outsells every other around the world?
candidates.note: Sold in 100+ countries, ~94% brand recognition — defeats the KitKat/Hershey reflex. Finalists Snickers, KitKat, Twix, Cadbury Dairy Milk.

candidates.q: Which is the best-selling candy in America that has no chocolate in it at all?
candidates.note: Took #1 around 2016-17. Finalists Skittles, Starburst, Sour Patch Kids, Twizzlers.

candidates.q: Which company sells more candy, by total revenue, than any other on Earth?
candidates.note: ~$54.6B, ahead of Mondelez (~$36B) and Ferrero (~$18B) — defeats the Hershey/Nestlé reflex. Finalists Mars, Mondelez, Ferrero, Nestlé, Hershey.

candidates.q: Which country sells more chocolate abroad — by value of exports — than any other?
candidates.note: Tops Belgium by export value (2023) — defeats the Switzerland/Belgium reflex. Finalists Germany, Belgium, Italy, Netherlands.

candidates.q: People in which country eat more chocolate per person than anywhere else?
candidates.note: ~8.8-11.6 kg/person/yr depending on source; Germany close behind. Flag: some lists put Luxembourg first. Finalists Switzerland, Germany, Austria, Belgium.

candidates.q: Which candy brand has been made without interruption in America longer than any other?
candidates.note: Flag: Necco Wafers date to 1847 but stopped 2018-2020, breaking continuity. Metric pinned to 'without interruption' for that reason. Finalists Good & Plenty, Tootsie Roll, Hershey bar, Necco.

candidates.q: Which brand of lollipop sells more than any other in the world?
candidates.note: ~12M/day across 150 countries; logo designed by Salvador Dalí. Finalists Chupa Chups, Tootsie Pop, Dum-Dums, Charms.

candidates.q: Which seasonal candy is churned out by the most sheer weight each year in the US?
candidates.note: ~35M lbs/yr (~9 billion kernels); Brach's makes ~30M of it. Finalists candy corn, Peeps, conversation hearts.

candidates.q: Which holiday sells more candy by weight in the US than any other?
candidates.note: ~$4.6B in candy; tops Easter, Christmas, Valentine's by volume. Flag: by the single busiest week, Easter narrowly wins. Finalists Halloween, Easter, Christmas, Valentine's Day.

candidates.q: People in which country eat more liquorice per person than anywhere else?
candidates.note: CONTESTED — Netherlands is frequently cited as #1 too (~2 kg), and salty 'salmiakki' is most beloved in Finland. A deliberately flagged-contested entry — want your read on whether these belong. Finalists Finland, Netherlands, Sweden, Denmark.

candidates.q: Which bite-sized chocolate is made in greater numbers every day than any other?
candidates.note: >70M per day across two factories, running 24/7. Finalists Hershey's Kisses, M&M's, Lindor truffles.
